ListItem.Para: Left ram air fan is deactivated.
Right ram air fan operates normally.
Right ram air fan motor controller operates normally.
Right air conditioning pack operates normally.
Right cabin air compressors operate normally.
L2 VFSG operates normally.
R2 VFSG operates normally.
R1 cabin air compressor CMSC operates normally.
R2 cabin air compressor CMSC operates normally.
Center hydraulic pump C2 operates normally.
Left PECS aft section operates normally.
Left PECS temperature sensor operates normally.
APU operates normally.
Ground operations are limited to OAT below 49 degrees C.

Note.Para: For an air conditioning pack to be operating normally, certain items associated with that system must operate normally. See MEL item 21-51-01 for a list of those items.
For a cabin air compressor to be operating normally, certain items must operate normally. See MEL item 21-52-01 for a list of those items.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the left ram air fan (AMM DDG 21-52-12).

ListItem.Para: Visually inspect the ram air fan blades for broken or damaged blades. If missing/damaged blades are found, visually inspect the inside and outside of the ram air outlet header for structural damage.
On the MISC SYSTEM CTRLS maintenance page, select RAM FANS LEFT OFF.

Note.Para: PACK L or PACK MODE L advisory message may be displayed.
PACK L or PACK MODE L advisory message may be displayed.

The left pack will be off and will not operate normally unless airspeed is sufficient to support ram air duct cooling (approximately 120 knots). The PACK L or PACK MODE L advisory message will be displayed when ram air duct cooling is not available.
Before engine start RAM FAN L, HYD PUMP C1, HYD PUMP DEM R and FUEL PUMP CENTER L status messages may be displayed.
For FCAC installed, FCAC performance may be degraded during ground operations. CARGO A/C FWD may be displayed when FWD CARGO A/C Selector is in the ON position.
Simultaneous engine start is not allowed.
ICS galley cooling performance may be degraded during ground operations if the SCUs are loadshed from the PECS.

On takeoff, the PACK ALTITUDE LIMIT caution message may appear anytime after liftoff until 1,000 feet above airport elevation. The message may be displayed for up to 2 minutes. On landing, this message may appear at altitudes less than 1000 feet above airport elevation until touchdown.

ListItem.Para: Ground operations are limited to OAT below 49 degrees C.
If cabin temperatures become warmer than desired, consider one or more of the following temporary actions:
Use pre-conditioned air to cool the cabin to 75 degrees F (24 degrees C) or less an hour before passenger loading.
Set IFE OFF using the IFE Power switch on the Video Control Station.
Select HEAT REDUCTION mode using the Cabin Attendant Panel TEMPERATURE menu.
For FCAC installed, select FCAC OFF.
To minimize PECS heating, operators should consider the following options:
Set packs OFF and use pre-conditioned air prior to engine start.
Delay closing forward or aft cargo doors until airplane is ready for push back or engine start.
Minimize APU ground operations.
Start APU prior to closing cargo doors.
Minimize time between cargo door closing and first engine start.
Each engine should be started individually at the gate.
On POWER ELEC COOLING maintenance page 1, confirm SUPPLY TEMP of left and right PECS loop is 142 degrees F (61 degrees C) or lower.
Start the APU.
Start first engine.

Note.Para: Engine start time will be longer than normal.

ListItem.Para: After first engine start has been completed, on POWER ELEC COOLING maintenance page 1, confirm SUPPLY TEMP of the left and right PECS loop is 142 degrees F (61 degrees C) or lower.
Start second engine.
Shutdown APU.
Within three minutes after second engine start, perform flight controls freedom of movement check and extend flaps.
Check status page for status messages.

Note.Para: ELEC ENGINE STARTER L1, ELEC ENGINE STARTER R1 and ELEC APU STARTER L status messages may be displayed. No action is required.

ListItem.Para: For HYD PUMP DEM R status message displayed:
Set R ENG PRIMARY pump switch OFF and R ELEC DEMAND pump selector ON until HYD PUMP DEM R status message is not displayed.
Set R ENG PRIMARY pump switch ON and R ELEC DEMAND selector AUTO.
For HYD PUMP C1 status message displayed:

Note.Para: If MEL 29-11-02 Center System EMP is applied, no action is required.

ListItem.Para: Set C2 ELEC pump selector OFF and C1 ELEC pump selector ON until HYD PUMP C1 status message is not displayed.
Set C1 and C2 ELEC pump selectors AUTO.
No further action is required for the following ram fan status messages:
RAM FAN L
RAM FAN PRIMARY L
RAM FAN CONTROL L
RAM FAN BACKUP L
RAM FAN BACKUP LEFT A
RAM FAN BACKUP LEFT B
Right ram air fan is deactivated.
Left ram air fan operates normally
Left ram air fan motor controller operates normally.
Left air conditioning pack operates normally.
Left cabin air compressors operate normally.
L1 VFSG operates normally.
R1 VFSG operates normally.
L1 cabin air compressor CMSC operates normally.
L2 cabin air compressor CMSC operates normally.
Center hydraulic pump C1 operates normally.
Right PECS aft section operates normally.
Right PECS temperature sensor operates normally.
APU operates normally.
Ground operations are limited to OAT below 49 degrees C.

Note.Para: For an air conditioning pack to be operating normally, certain items associated with that system must operate normally. See MEL item 21-51-01 for a list of those items.
For a cabin air compressor to be operating normally, certain items must operate normally. See MEL item 21-52-01 for a list of those items.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the right ram air fan (AMM DDG 21-52-12).

ListItem.Para: Visually inspect the ram air fan blades for broken or damaged blades. If missing/damaged blades are found, visually inspect the inside and outside of the ram air outlet header for structural damage.
On the MISC SYSTEM CTRLS maintenance page, select RAM FANS RIGHT OFF.

Note.Para: PACK R or PACK MODE R advisory message may be displayed.
PACK R or PACK MODE R advisory message may be displayed.

The right pack will be off and will not operate normally unless airspeed is sufficient to support ram air duct cooling (approximately 120 knots). The PACK R or PACK MODE R advisory message will be displayed when ram air duct cooling is not available.
Before engine start RAM FAN R, HYD PUMP C2 HYD PUMP DEM L, and FUEL PUMP CENTER R status messages may be displayed.
For FCAC installed, FCAC performance may be degraded during ground operations. CARGO A/C FWD may be displayed when FWD CARGO A/C Selector is in the ON position.
Simultaneous engine start is not allowed.
ICS galley cooling performance may be degraded during ground operations if the SCUs are loadshed from the PECS

On takeoff, the PACK ALTITUDE LIMIT caution message may appear anytime after liftoff until 1,000 feet above airport elevation. The message may be displayed for up to 2 minutes. On landing, this message may appear at altitudes less than 1000 feet above airport elevation until touchdown.

ListItem.Para: Ground operations are limited to OAT below 49 degrees C.
If cabin temperatures become warmer than desired, consider one or more of the following temporary actions:
Use pre-conditioned air to cool the cabin to 75 degrees F (24 degrees C) or less an hour before passenger loading.
Set IFE OFF using the IFE Power switch on the Video Control Station.
Select HEAT REDUCTION mode using the Cabin Attendant Panel TEMPERATURE menu.
For FCAC installed, select FCAC OFF
To minimize PECS heating, operators should consider the following options:
Set packs OFF and use pre-conditioned air prior to engine start.
Delay closing forward or aft cargo doors until airplane is ready for push back or engine start.
Minimize APU ground operations.
Start APU prior to closing cargo doors.
Minimize time between cargo door closing and first engine start.
Each engine should be started individually at the gate.
On POWER ELEC COOLING maintenance page 1, confirm SUPPLY TEMP of right and left PECS loop is 142 degrees F (61 degrees C) or lower.
Start the APU.
Start first engine.

Note.Para: Engine start time will be longer than normal.

ListItem.Para: After first engine start has been completed, on POWER ELEC COOLING maintenance page 1, confirm SUPPLY TEMP of the right and left PECS loop is 142 degrees F (61 degrees C) or lower.
Start second engine.
Shutdown APU.
Within three minutes after second engine start, perform flight controls freedom of movement check and extend flaps.
Check status page for status messages.

Note.Para: ELEC ENGINE STARTER L2, ELEC ENGINE STARTER R2 and ELEC APU STARTER R status messages may be displayed. No action is required.

ListItem.Para: For HYD PUMP DEM L status message displayed:
Set L ENG PRIMARY pump switch OFF and L ELEC DEMAND pump selector ON until HYD PUMP DEM L status message is not displayed.
Set L ENG PRIMARY pump switch ON and L ELEC DEMAND pump selector AUTO.
For HYD PUMP C2 status message displayed:

Note.Para: If MEL 29-11-02 Center System EMP is applied, no action is required.

ListItem.Para: Set C1 ELEC pump selector OFF and C2 ELEC pump selector ON until HYD PUMP C2 status message is not displayed.
Set C1 and C2 ELEC pump selectors AUTO.
No further action is required for the following ram fan status messages:
RAM FAN R
RAM FAN PRIMARY R
RAM FAN CONTROL R
RAM FAN BACKUP R
RAM FAN DUAL CONTROL

MELSubItem.Title: Left Ram Air Fan Motor Controller

ListItem.Para: Right ram air fan operates normally.
Right ram air fan motor controller operates normally.
L2 cabin air compressor CMSC operates normally.
L2 VFSG operates normally.
R2 VFSG operates normally.
Left ram inlet door or ram air exit door operates normally.

Note.Para: Status message RAM FAN DUAL CONTROL will also be displayed. The airplane must also be dispatched using MEL item 21-52-12-05.
Status message CABIN TEMP CONTROL L will also be displayed.

Dispatch is allowed with INOP RFMC installed if L1 ATRUC does not trip when circuit breaker MC-RAM Fan L CHAN 1 is open. To avoid L1 ATRUC trip, lock with an INOP tag circuit breaker MC-RAM FAN L CHAN 1 to remove L RFMC 28V power. Dispatch is not allowed with the ATRUC tripped.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: A VFSG system is considered to be operating normally if its associated status message (ELEC GEN SYS L1, L2, R1 or R2) is not displayed.
An inoperative ram fan motor controller (RFMC) may be removed provided dispatch tool K24002 is installed (AMM DDG 21-52-12).

ListItem.Para: Remove the ram fan motor controller.
Open the following circuit breakers and lock with DO NOT CLOSE tags:
MC-RAM FAN L CHAN 1
LIQUID CLG PUMP-L1
LIQUID CLG PUMP-L2
Open the following contactors and lock with DO NOT CLOSE tags:
ATRUC-L1
ATRUC-L2
Gain access to the P700 panel.
Disconnect and set swing bolts clear of RFMC mounting brackets.
Remove RFMC from the EE tray.
Install plugs on RFMC liquid connector.
Install RFMC dispatch tool.
Remove necessary plugs and caps on the RFMC dispatch tool.
Carefully install dispatch tool in the RFMC E/E tray.
Install swing bolts in dispatch tool mounting brackets and tighten if necessary.
Close the following circuit breakers:
MC-RAM FAN L CHAN 1
LIQUID CLG PUMP-L1
LIQUID CLG PUMP-L2

Note.Para: The following steps are required to reset the ATRUC contactors to AUTO.

ListItem.Para: Set the following contactors to AUTO:
ATRUC-L1
ATRUC-L2
Lock the following contactors with DO NOT CLOSE tags:
ATRUC-L1
ATRUC-L2
Set the following contactors to AUTO:
ATRUC-L1
ATRUC-L2
Momentarily push the AFT EXT PWR switch on the P5 overhead panel.

Note.Para: Engine start time will be longer than normal.
PACK MODE L, ELEC MC HYD R, ELEC ENGINE STARTER L1, ELEC ENGINE STARTER R1 may be displayed during engine start.
Simultaneous engine start is not allowed.

On takeoff, the PACK ALTITUDE LIMIT caution message may appear anytime after liftoff until 1,000 feet above airport elevation. The message may be displayed for up to 2 minutes. On landing, this message may appear at altitudes less than 1000 feet above airport elevation until touchdown.

MELSubItem.Title: Right Ram Air Fan Motor Controller

ListItem.Para: Left ram air fan operates normally.
Left ram air fan motor controller operates normally.
R2 cabin air compressor CMSC operates normally.
L1 VFSG operates normally.
R1 VFSG operates normally.
Right ram inlet door or ram air exit door operates normally.

Note.Para: Status message RAM FAN DUAL CONTROL will also be displayed. The airplane must also be dispatched using MEL item 21-52-12-05.
Status message CABIN TEMP CONTROL L will also be displayed.

Dispatch is allowed with INOP RFMC installed if R1 ATRUC does not trip when circuit breaker MC-RAM FAN R CHAN 1 is open. To avoid R1 ATRUC trip, lock with an INOP tag circuit breaker MC-RAM FAN R CHAN 1 to remove R RFMC 28V power. Dispatch is not allowed with the ATRUC tripped.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: A VFSG system is considered to be operating normally if its associated status message (ELEC GEN SYS L1, L2, R1 or R2) is not displayed.
An inoperative ram fan motor controller (RFMC) may be removed provided dispatch tool K24002 is installed (AMM DDG 21-52-12).

ListItem.Para: Remove the ram fan motor controller.
Open the following circuit breakers and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag:
MC-RAM FAN R CHAN 1
LIQUID CLG PUMP-R1
LIQUID CLG PUMP-R2
Open the following contactors and lock with DO NOT CLOSE tags:
ATRUC-R1
ATRUC-R2
Gain access to the P800 panel.
Disconnect and set swing bolts clear of RFMC mounting brackets.
Remove RFMC from the EE tray.
Install plugs on RFMC liquid connector.
Install RFMC dispatch tool.
Remove necessary plugs and caps on the RFMC dispatch tool.
Carefully install dispatch tool in the RFMC E/E tray.
Install swing bolts in dispatch tool mounting brackets and tighten if necessary.
Close the following circuit breakers:
MC-RAM FAN R CHAN 1
LIQUID CLG PUMP-R1
LIQUID CLG PUMP-R2

Note.Para: The following steps are required to reset the ATRUC contactors to AUTO.

ListItem.Para: Set the following contactors to AUTO:
ATRUC-R1
ATRUC-R2
Lock the following contactors with DO NOT CLOSE tags:
ATRUC-R1
ATRUC-R2
Set the following contactors to AUTO:
ATRUC-R1
ATRUC-R2
Momentarily push the AFT EXT PWR switch on the P5 overhead panel.

Note.Para: Engine start time will be longer than normal.
PACK MODE L, ELEC MC HYD R, ELEC ENGINE STARTER L1, ELEC ENGINE STARTER R1 may be displayed during engine start.
Simultaneous engine start is not allowed.

On takeoff, the PACK ALTITUDE LIMIT caution message may appear anytime after liftoff until 1,000 feet above airport elevation. The message may be displayed for up to 2 minutes. On landing, this message may appear at altitudes less than 1000 feet above airport elevation until touchdown.

ListItem.Para: If cabin temperatures become warmer than desired, consider one or more of the following temporary actions:
Select IFE system modes designed to reduce cabin heat loads.

Note.Para: Availability of modes dependent on IFE system installed and airline modifiable options.

ListItem.Para: Set IFE OFF using the IFE Power switch on the Video Control Station.
Select HEAT REDUCTION mode using the Cabin Attendant Panel TEMPERATURE menu (adjusts cabin lighting and window shading).
For FCAC installed, set FCAC OFF.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided air conditioning packs operate normally.

MaintenanceNote.Para: For an air conditioning pack to be operating normally, certain items must operate normally. See MEL item 21-51-01 for a list of those items.
